old_claim.note: crispr_gene_editing was previously treated as a plausible hard prerequisite for pam_specificity_engineering without a field-specific edge receipt.

new_claim.note: Wild-type CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing exposed PAM targeting constraints that engineered PAM-specificity variants were designed to relax.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and engineering workflow describing Cas9 variants with altered PAM specificities for expanding CRISPR-Cas9 targeting range.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper frames altered PAM-specificity engineering as modifying CRISPR-Cas9 nucleases to relax targeting limits in CRISPR genome-editing use.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The dependency is on the CRISPR-Cas9 editing platform whose targeting constraint motivates the engineered variants, not on generic protein mutation alone.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: pam_specificity_engineering -> crispr_gene_editing remains required; this receipt adds source-shaped evidence for the existing CRISPR field edge.
would_reject_if: The source concerned non-CRISPR nucleases rather than CRISPR-Cas9 editing.
The node was rescoped to all DNA-binding specificity engineering while retaining this hard CRISPR edge.
The edge note no longer tied altered PAM specificity to CRISPR-Cas9 editing constraints.
short_reason: Altered PAM variants are engineered to extend CRISPR-Cas9 genome-editing targetability.
